posts.name: William Kelley

posts.name: Michael S. Monie
posts.cooked: Both WINEMAN and AMARONE have 7 letters, so would fit. But it seems a bad idea to advertise to law enforcement that one is an alcohol enthusiast.

posts.name: Chris Seiber
posts.cooked:
Both WINEMAN and AMARONE have 7 letters, so would fit. But it seems a bad idea to advertise to law enforcement that one is an alcohol enthusiast.

Reminds me of when running back Knowshon Moreno got a DUI, and his personalized license plate was “SAUCED”

https://www.google.com/amp/s/www.chicagotribune.com/sports/ct-xpm-2012-02-06-chi-report-broncos-moreno-arrested-for-dui-20120206-story,amp.html

But I don’t know that a policeman is going to have a reaction to AMARONE or WHT BURG or something like that.

I saw a car once with 61 LATOUR as the plate.

posts.cooked: Mine is “BAROLO”

My plate is WHT BURG

posts.name: Todd French

BRSRK

Just kidding. No vanity plate.
I have seen a car in town with ‘MERLOT’.

posts.name: Patrick Duffy
posts.cooked: Oregon has available special plates that have “WC,” for “Wine Country,” on the left, before the specific numbers/letters. I got a lot of points with my wife when I got her WC plates with her name.
